title: Solutions, challenges and rising tensions in AI and mathematics - Nature Machine Intelligence
gist: AI math reasoning has accelerated sharply since 2024, with DeepMind's AlphaProof hitting IMO silver-medal level and an OpenAI model disproving a long-standing Erdos conjecture via a non-human approach. The piece flags rising tension: the Leiden Declaration warns tech-company involvement and AI 'slop' could erode verification, attribution, and research autonomy in mathematics.

insights.claim: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof, a generalist formal reasoning model combining LLM reasoning and reinforcement learning, achieved silver-medal-level performance at the 2024 Mathematical Olympiad by solving four of six problems, one point short of gold.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: Olympiad-level formal reasoning is a capability milestone that AI labs use as a competitive showcase, signaling rapid progress in verifiable reasoning.
insights.quote: solving four out of six problems and falling one point short of the gold-medal range

insights.claim: An internal OpenAI reasoning model disproved a central conjecture related to the 80-year-old unit distance problem in combinatorial geometry, and appeared to pursue a different approach from those typically explored by human mathematicians.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: AI resolving a famous open problem via a non-human path suggests models may explore solution spaces humans neglect, a qualitative shift in research value.
insights.quote: the model appears to have pursued a different approach from those typically explored by human mathematicians
insights.locator: Most recently, an internal OpenAI reasoning model

insights.claim: Google DeepMind reported that AlphaProof Nexus resolved nine additional open Erdos problems.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: A run of open-problem resolutions indicates AI is moving from solving known-answer benchmarks to generating genuinely new mathematical results.
insights.locator: Around the same time, Google DeepMind reported

insights.claim: The neuro-symbolic system TongGeometry solved all 30 problems in a Mathematical Olympiad geometry benchmark and proposed several problems selected for national competitions in 2024.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: AI not only solving but proposing competition-grade problems shows generative capability beyond answer production, relevant to neuro-symbolic approaches.
insights.quote: solved all 30 problems in a Mathematical Olympiad geometry benchmark problem dataset
insights.locator: In related work, Zhang et al.

insights.claim: Free boundary neural operators have been developed to solve partial differential equations when the solution domain is unknown, extending scientific machine learning to problems involving irreversible, catastrophic transitions like glacial melting and tumour growth.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: Extending neural PDE solvers to free boundary problems opens high-value modeling markets in climate and oncology where domains shift dynamically.
insights.locator: Furthermore, in a recent breakthrough, Long et al.

insights.claim: Workflows for checking and independently verifying mathematical output are not keeping pace with the increasing use of AI technology.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: A verification bottleneck means AI-generated math results may outrun the field's ability to validate them, a risk to research integrity and a tooling opportunity.
insights.quote: workflows for checking and independently verifying output are not keeping pace

insights.claim: Mathematicians are increasingly worried about the rise of AI 'slop' and the growing involvement of technology companies in mathematical research, which may introduce skewed incentives.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: Skewed incentives from corporate involvement could distort which problems get prioritized, a governance risk as AI labs use math as a marketing showcase.
insights.quote: the growing involvement of technology companies in mathematical research, which may introduce skewed incentives

insights.claim: The Leiden Declaration, an open letter from international mathematicians, warns AI overuse could undermine transparency, rigorous independent verification, and proper attribution of credit, and calls for disclosure of AI tool use and recognition of human contributors.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: A formal declaration signals emerging norms and possible disclosure requirements for AI use in research, a regulatory direction to watch.

insights.claim: The Leiden Declaration argues researchers must retain freedom to decide whether and how to incorporate AI to avoid the erosion of research autonomy.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: Research-autonomy concerns extend beyond math and foreshadow broader debates over AI dependence across scientific disciplines.
insights.quote: the need to avoid the erosion of research autonomy
